summary: Officially documented crash-recovery control for automatic restart after a system failure. Microsoft documents the same CrashControl path and AutoReboot value used by the app, including the default enabled state and the 0 = disabled troubleshooting state.

setting.name: Automatic Restart on System Failure
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can automatically restart the PC after a blue screen. Turning that off gives you time to see the crash screen and stop code before the machine reboots.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable automatic restart when they are diagnosing crashes and want the blue screen to stay visible instead of disappearing immediately.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off helps troubleshooting, but it also means the machine stays down after a crash until someone restarts it manually.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents the Startup and Recovery option under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\CrashControl. The Windows Client troubleshooting guidance says AutoReboot is on by default and that setting AutoReboot to 0 disables automatic restart. Microsoft also publishes AutoReboot as a Win32_OSRecoveryConfiguration property mapped to the same registry value.

allowed_values.label: Automatic restart disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not automatically reboot after a system failure.

allowed_values.label: Automatic restart enabled
allowed_values.meaning: Windows automatically reboots after a system failure.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes AutoReboot = 0, which matches the documented disabled state.

windows_defaults.label: Windows default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems using the normal crash-recovery baseline

states.rationale: Microsoft documents automatic restart as turned on by default.

windows_defaults.notes: This is the normal Startup and Recovery baseline for Windows.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems where automatic recovery matters more than on-screen diagnostics

states.rationale: Keep automatic restart on after a crash.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the machine to recover from a crash by rebooting automatically.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need the blue screen to remain visible for diagnosis.
tradeoffs: The system reboots automatically after a crash.
The blue screen can disappear before you read the stop code.

recommended_profiles.label: Diagnostics visible
avoid_for: Unattended or kiosk-style systems that should restart automatically

states.rationale: Keep the crash screen visible until a person restarts the machine.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You are diagnosing crashes and want time to read the stop code or photograph the blue screen.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the machine to recover by itself after a crash.
tradeoffs: The blue screen remains visible for diagnosis.
The machine stays down until someone restarts it.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Configure system failure and recovery options
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents CrashControl as the registry surface for Startup and Recovery options, states that automatic restart is on by default, and says setting AutoReboot to 0 turns it off.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Memory dump file options
evidence.summary: Microsoft lists AutoReboot REG_DWORD 0x1 as an additional CrashControl registry value for Startup and Recovery.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Win32_OSRecoveryConfiguration
evidence.summary: Microsoft maps the AutoReboot WMI property directly to SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\CrashControl\AutoReboot.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes AutoReboot = 0 for the current tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the exact CrashControl registry surface for Startup and Recovery, states that automatic restart is on by default, and says setting AutoReboot to 0 turns it off. The app writes that exact troubleshooting value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\CrashControl ... AutoReboot REG_DWORD 0x1; to disable automatic restart set the AutoReboot DWORD to 0.
validation_proof.notes: The Microsoft troubleshooting page explicitly names the CrashControl registry path, lists AutoReboot as a REG_DWORD with default 0x1, and states that setting AutoReboot to 0 disables automatic restart.
